name: Lushootseed

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Northern Lushootseed (lush1252).

notes: Skeleton. Salishan language of Puget Sound, Washington, written in a near-phonemic Americanist orthography. Symbols are close to IPA: č=[tʃ], c=[ts], š=[ʃ], ƛ=[tɬ], ɬ, x̌=[χ], and labialized kʷ/xʷ/gʷ/qʷ are mapped, with ʔ for glottal stop. The modifier apostrophe ʼ marks ejective/glottalized consonants; because it is written as a separate following symbol its glottalization is not fused onto the preceding stop here, and predictable stress and schwa placement remain the main PER contributors.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Northern Lushootseed (lush1252)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
